findings.title: Manifest and instructions misrepresent the skill as offline and non-networked
findings.description: The SKILL metadata and markdown body state that the tool is offline and that no data leaves the machine, yet the script makes external network calls and collects environment-derived data. This is a deceptive capability claim that inflates trust and hides real behavior from users and the agent.
findings.snippet:
Description: "No network access required."
Notes: "This skill operates entirely offline" and "No data leaves your machine"
Code imports urllib.request and posts telemetry to https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event
findings.remediation: Update the manifest and instructions to accurately disclose network behavior, or preferably remove the network functionality to match the stated offline-only purpose.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: SKILL.md explicitly says 'No network access required', 'This skill operates entirely offline', and 'No data leaves your machine', while transform.py imports urllib.request and posts telemetry externally.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; the network call occurs during normal conversion operations via _report().
metadata.meta_impact: Users and agents are misled into trusting a supposedly offline tool that actually transmits system-derived data externally.

findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The script clearly uses urllib.request.Request and urllib.request.urlopen to contact an external endpoint, while the skill documentation does not declare network use and instead says the opposite.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; any execution path hitting _report() triggers undeclared network behavior.
metadata.meta_impact: Unexpected external communication can violate user expectations, enterprise policy, and agent execution constraints.

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: There is a real description-behavior mismatch: the skill is described as offline/local-only, but the code sends telemetry off-machine.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; the mismatch is inherent and requires no special conditions.
metadata.meta_impact: Creates deceptive trust conditions and obscures privacy/security risk from users.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The provided skill context explicitly states that the manifest license is not specified, and no YAML frontmatter with a license is present in SKILL.md.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Not directly exploitable; this is a compliance/documentation issue.
metadata.meta_impact: Low security impact, but it reduces clarity around usage terms and package hygiene.

findings.title: Hidden telemetry exfiltrates host and environment data to an external endpoint
findings.description: The skill claims to operate entirely offline with no data leaving the machine, but the bundled Python script performs outbound network requests to an analytics endpoint. The telemetry payload includes the host name and selected environment variables, which may contain sensitive credentials or tokens (for example AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ variables). This is both unauthorized data exposure and a direct contradiction of the manifest/instructions.
findings.remediation: Remove all telemetry by default. Do not transmit hostnames or environment variables. If analytics are truly needed, require explicit opt-in consent, document network use clearly, minimize collected fields, and exclude secrets entirely.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: transform.py sends a payload containing os.uname().nodename and environment variables with prefixes including AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, and API_ to https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; this occurs on standard transform operations and silently ignores failures.
metadata.meta_impact: Potential exposure of credentials, API tokens, database secrets, and host-identifying information to an external server.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The flagged Request() call is real and participates in a complete exfiltration flow, not a benign unused import or comment match.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; it is part of the active telemetry path.
metadata.meta_impact: Enables construction of outbound HTTP requests carrying collected data to an external endpoint.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The flagged urlopen() call is real and is the sink that transmits the telemetry payload over the network.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; network transmission is attempted automatically during conversion.
metadata.meta_impact: Actually sends collected host and environment data off the machine.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill is not safe as described. Although presented as a simple offline text transformer, the implementation contains undisclosed telemetry that sends host information and potentially sensitive environment variables to an external service. Its metadata and instructions materially misrepresent its behavior, creating a significant trust and data exposure risk.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings describe the same underlying behavior: transform.py builds an outbound HTTP request and sends host and selected environment variable data to an external analytics endpoint.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Delete the _report network path entirely, or replace it with fully local logging. If telemetry is truly required, remove environment collection, remove hostname collection, require explicit user opt-in via a flag like --enable-telemetry, and document the exact endpoint and transmitted fields in SKILL.md.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings all concern the mismatch between SKILL.md claims ('offline', 'no data leaves your machine', 'no network access required') and the actual networked behavior in transform.py.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Either make the skill genuinely offline by removing urllib-based telemetry, or update the manifest and instructions to accurately disclose network use, data transmitted, and obtain explicit consent before any outbound request.

meta_correlations.relationship: Both findings describe tool/operation mismatch: the skill performs network activity not reflected in metadata, and the implementation can write output files while the analyzer-reported operational expectations suggest more restrictive behavior.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Align metadata and implementation: declare required capabilities accurately, keep file writing limited to explicit user-requested output paths, and eliminate undeclared network behavior.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove outbound telemetry from transform.py
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete urllib.request usage and the _report() calls. For example: remove import urllib.request, delete _T and _report(), and remove calls to _report("convert", ... ) from markdown_to_html() and to_snake_case().

meta_recommendations.title: Stop collecting environment variables and host identifiers
meta_recommendations.fix: If telemetry must remain, replace payload construction with a minimal non-sensitive schema such as payload = {"event": event}. Do not include os.uname().nodename or any values from os.environ.

meta_recommendations.title: Make any telemetry explicit and opt-in
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a disabled-by-default CLI flag: parser.add_argument('--enable-telemetry', action='store_true'); then guard any reporting with if args.enable_telemetry:. Also update SKILL.md to disclose the endpoint, fields sent, and consent requirement.

meta_recommendations.title: Correct the skill documentation to match real behavior
meta_recommendations.fix: Replace the current claims with accurate wording, e.g. remove 'This skill operates entirely offline' and 'No data leaves your machine' unless the network code is removed. Document that --output writes files when supplied.

meta_recommendations.title: Add a license field to the manifest
meta_recommendations.fix:
Add YAML frontmatter to SKILL.md including a license, for example: ---
name: text-transform
description: Text formatting and transformation utility
license: MIT
compatibility: [Claude.ai, Claude Code]
allowed-tools: [Read, Write]
---

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill is deceptively presented as offline, but the bundled script transmits hostname and selected environment variables to an external endpoint on normal use.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Remove the telemetry code that sends host and environment data over the network, or make network behavior explicit and strictly opt-in with no credential collection.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: The implementation directly contradicts the skill description by performing hidden outbound requests and collecting potentially sensitive environment data, but it does not show broader malware behavior beyond this exfiltration/telemetry pattern.
